key_insight: Physics-based causal AI can disentangle how electrode composition and loading independently affect electrocatalyst performance, even in very small multi-modal datasets.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical route to optimize ink and electrode preparation variables by linking material formulation decisions to device-level electrochemical outcomes.
method: Built a physics-based structural causal model (SCM) for heterogeneous electrochemical data.
Analyzed cyclic voltammograms of Mn-Sb oxide oxygen-reduction catalysts on Vulcan carbon in alkaline media.
Separated causal effects of support-to-catalyst ratio, total loading, active-site count, and electrode thickness.
evidence_signal: The proof-of-concept uses small datasets (n < 10) with more than 10 data modes to quantitatively interpret cyclic voltammograms and performance drivers.
watch_item: The approach is demonstrated on a preliminary, small-scale dataset, so broader validation is needed before generalizing to other catalyst systems or electrode formats.
paper_title: Bridging electrode preparation and electrocatalyst performance with physics-based causal AI
